name: bespunky-voice
description: Hear Claude's questions without reading the screen. When Claude asks something, get a listenable, audio-dedicated summary — no markup or structure read aloud, just the gist and the options — so you can decide while away from the keyboard. Two triggers: MANUAL (`/voice say` reads the current question on demand; plus `/voice test`, `/voice status`) and AUTOMATIC (`/voice auto on` — a PreToolUse hook then speaks every multiple-choice question and plan-approval as it appears; default off, so nothing is spoken unless you opt in; for free-text questions use `/voice say`). TTS sits behind a swappable boundary: a free espeak-ng floor on Linux and `say` on macOS, auto-upgraded to a local Piper neural voice when installed (scripts/install-piper.sh), with the boundary built to allow a cloud voice too — the plugin decides WHEN and WHAT to speak, not which engine. Needs a synthesis engine (espeak-ng / macOS say / Piper), a player (paplay, aplay, or afplay), Node, and a reachable audio sink; in the BeSpunky devcontainer that is WSLg's PULSE_SERVER. This is the speak-half; answering by voice (speech-to-text) is a planned later tier, gated on microphone input.
